UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS JAMES YOUNG AND BETTY YOUNG'S AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO NOV. 7, 2012 9:30AM. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED.

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS JAMES YOUNG, AND BETTY YOUNG'S AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SHOW THAT SERVICE OF NOTICE OF RENT INCREASE WAS "IN CONNECTION WITH A PUBLIC ISSUE OR AN ISSUE OF PUBLIC INTEREST." MOVING PARTY CONCEDES THAT THERE ARE NO LEGAL AUTHORITIES ON POINT. REPLY 2:22-23. NOTICE INVOLVED INTERESTS OF ONE LANDLORD AND ONE TENANT IN CONNECTION TO ONE PROPERTY. BUT FOR THIS LITIGATION NOTICE WOULD NOT BE PUBLISHED TO ANY THIRD PARTIES. THUS, SERVICE OF NOTICE COULD NOT DIRECTLY EFFECT A LARGE NUMBER OF PEOPLE BEYOND THE DIRECT PARTICIPANTS. ASSERTION OF BROAD AND AMORPHOUS PUBLIC INTEREST TO PAST CONTROVERSY INVOLVING COSTA-HAWKINS ACT IS INSUFFICIENT; MOVING PARTY DOES NOT ASSERT THAT THERE IS A PRESENT WIDE-SPREAD INTEREST IN COSTA-HAWKINS. REPLY 5:16. MOVING PARTY ALSO DOES NOT EXPLAIN HOW ITS NOTICE CONTRIBUTED TO THIS PAST CONTROVERSY OR WAS EVEN CONNECTED TO IT. FINALLY, THE FOCUS OF MOVING PARTY'S CONDUCT WAS RENT INCREASE FOR ONE TENANT, NOT THE VALIDITY OF RENT INCREASE STATUTES. SEE CROSS V. COOPER (2011) 197 CAL.APP.4TH 357; WEINBERG V. FEISEL (2003) 110 CAL.APP.4TH 1122; AND WILBANKS V. WOLK (2004) 121 CAL.APP.4TH 883. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE RONALD E QUIDACHAY, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-19-2013 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-04-2013 Continued To Mar-06-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-13-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances - Atty. Jonathan Joiner of Law Offices of Richard Hurlburt (415-391-6496) opposing party. No other appearance.

The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTs JAMES YOUNG, and BETTY YOUNG's AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is Denied. Moving party failed to show that service of notice of rent increase was "in connection with a public issue or an issue of public interest." Moving party concedes that there are no legal authorities on point. Reply 2:22-23. Notice involved interests of one landlord and one tenant in connection to one property. But for this litigation Notice would not be published to any third parties. Thus, service of Notice could not directly effect a large number of people beyond the direct participants. Assertion of broad and amorphous public interest to past controversy involving Costa-Hawkins Act is insufficient; moving party does not assert that there is a present wide-spread interest in Costa-Hawkins. Reply 5:16. Moving party also does not explain how its Notice contributed to this past controversy or was even connected to it. Finally, the focus of moving party's conduct was rent increase for one tenant, not the validity of rent increase statutes. See Cross v. Cooper (2011) 197 Cal.App.4th 357; Weinberg v. Feisel (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1122; and Wilbanks v. Wolk (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 883. Order signed in open court. Judge Ronald E Quidachay, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (501/REQ)

AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

ARGUED - Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument takes DEFENDANTs JAMES YOUNG and BETTY YOUNG's AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT under submission. Defendant to submit an order to the Court granting the motion. Plaintiff to submit and order denying the motion. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Smith, CSR: Barrie Hart #6954 (415-328-6644) (D501)
